meaning: n.破口,崩溃处,裂缝vt.使有裂缝
examples: A crevasse yawned at their feet.
He watched as nine people were swept into the crevasse.
The Japanese government hasn't taken decisive action to restimulate an economy and banking system that is teetering on a crevasse.
Mr Legate lost his footing while seeking to fix ropes and fell about 200 metres (655 feet) into a crevasse, the Nepalese ministry said.
